english: (Ssk. प्रधान, consequently = mčog (mchog)) 1. the highest in perfection, the most excellent in its kind, gtsó-bor (gtso bor) or gtsor byéd-pa (gtsor byed pa), lén-pa (len pa) to place foremost, to consider the first or most excellent; gtso byás-pai bú-mo lṅa (gtso byas pa'i bu mo lnga) the five noblest of the girls Mil.; gtso byéd-pa-rnams (gtso byed pa rnams) the most respectable, the leaders, the heads Mil.; des gtsó-byas dpon-yyóg-rnams (des gtso byas dpon g.yog rnams) the higher and lower people subject to him Pth. (gtsó-byed-pa (gtso byed pa) to be the first, belongs however rather under the head of no. 2); sṅágs-kyi gtsó-bo (sngags kyi gtso bo), smón-lam-gyi gtsó-bo (smon lam gyi gtso bo) (the same as rgyál-po (rgyal po)) chief spell, principal prayer; yi-ge gtsó-bo súm-ču (yi ge gtso bo sum cu) the 30 principal letters, (the letters of the alphabet) Glr.; nad-rnams kún-gyi gtsó-bo (nad rnams kun gyi gtso bo) the principal disease, viz. fever Lt. (more correct from an Indian than from a Tibetan point of view); gtsó-čé-ba (gtso che ba) very important Thgr.; eminent Tar.; gtsó-bor (gtso bor) and gtsó-čér (gtso cher), adv., especially, chiefly, principally. Hence: 2. a chief, a principal, master, lord, rkaṅ-yñís-kyi (rkang gnyis kyi) (lord) of men, i.e. Buddha Dzl.; rtén-gyi gtso-bo (rten gyi gtso bo) the 'lord' of the shrine, the deity to whom a shrine is consecrated, which in the lord's absence is guarded by some servant deity, e.g. Dzl. chap. VI.; čhós-kyi gtsó-bo čén-po (chos kyi gtso bo chen po) grand-master of the doctrine, a title of Sariibu Dzl.; gentleman, but chiefly as a title = Sir, Mr., blón-po gtsó-bo drúg-po (blon po gtso bo drug po), gtsó-bo mi drúg (gtso bo mi drug) the six (gentlemen) ministers Glr.; gtsó-mo (gtso mo) the most distinguished lady, the noblest, first in rank, bú-mo gtsó-mo (bu mo gtso mo) the most excellent among the girls; gtsó-mor 'ós-pa žig (gtso mor 'os pa zhig) the one most deserving of preference, the one of the noblest appearance Mil.; gtsó-mo mdzád-pa (gtso mo mdzad pa) to be mistress, resp.

(Ld. vulgo *stsod*), the so-called Tibetan antelope, with straight horns standing close together and in the direction of the longitudinal axis of the head S.g., gtsód-mo (gtsod mo) fem., gtsód-p'rug (gtsod phrug) the young one, gtsód-rus (gtsod rus) the bones, gtsód-k'ul (gtsod khul) the wool of it (used for shawls).

(btsa-ba (btsa ba) Sch.?) 1. rust, lčágs-kyi btsa (lcags kyi btsa') rust of iron; btsas-zas (btsas zas) Sch., k'yer (khyer) Lex. destroyed by rust. - 2. rust, blight, smut, of corn Sch. - 3. = btsag (btsag), Sch. - me-btsá (me btsa') moxa Lt.; mi-rus-btsa (mi rus btsa')?

1. pf. btsas (btsas), to bear, to bring forth, čuṅ-ma-la bu btsas (chung ma la bu btsas) his wife bore, gave birth to, a son Dzl.; bu btsá-bai t'abs mi t'ub (bu btsa' ba'i thabs mi thub) they could not bring forth Dzl.; btsás-pa (btsas pa) what is begotten, new-born children or animals Do.; btsá-zug laṅs (btsa' zug langs) pains of labour ensued Sch. - 2. resp. to watch, look on, spy, spyán-gyis (spyan gyis) C.s.

(Ssk. गैरिक), red ochre Med. and Lex.; used also of earths of a different colour; btsag-t'áṅ (btsag thang), btsag-ri (btsag ri), btsag-luṅ (btsag lung) plain, hill, valley, of red earth; btsag-yug (btsag yug) some other officinal mineral Med.
title of sovereigns Glr., alledged to be but Khams-dialect for btsán-po (btsan po).
1. a species of demons, residing in the air, on high rocks etc., mischievous, Glr., Dom. - 2. v. the following article.

strong, mighty, powerful, of kings, ministers etc., esp. as title of honour: high-potent, Dzl., Glr.; hence of family, race, descent: illustrious, noble, lhá-mo btsán-rnams (lha mo btsan rnams) the queens of high descent, in opp. to a third of low extraction Glr.; btsan-(žiṅ) p'yug(-po) (btsan (zhing) phyug (po)) noble and rich Dzl., Mil.; strong, violent, btsan-dúg (btsan dug) a virulent poison Dzl.; forcible, violent, btsan-p'rógs byéd-pa (btsan phrogs byed pa) to commit a robbery connected with violence Pth.; btsan-t'abs-su (btsan thabs su) by violent means Pth.; coercive, strict, severe bka (bka'), k'rims (khrims) Glr., btsán-par mdzád-pa (btsan par mdzad pa) rigorously to enforce (a law); firm, staunch, immovable, not wavering, ṅag-btsán (ngag btsan) steadfastly abiding by one's word Sch.; firm, safe.
